varieties.name: Seedling Selections (Queensland-Adapted)
varieties.origin: Queensland, Australia (grown from diverse tropical seed sources)
varieties.traits: Heart-shaped fruits, dark green skin with soft fleshy spines, fruit size 10-30cm long, weight 1.5-4kg
varieties.flesh_fruit: White, juicy, slightly fibrous, creamy texture with sweet-tart flavor combining strawberry, pineapple and citrus notes
varieties.yield_notes: 12-24 fruits per tree annually, fruit production begins 2-3 years from seed in tropical conditions, 3-5 years in subtropics
varieties.growing_notes: Requires frost-free site with protection from cold winds. In Queensland, well-watered trees reach 4.5-5.5m in 6-7 years. Fruiting season begins April. Sensitive to temperatures below 5°C when young, requiring protection until established. Main pest is mealybug on fruits. Prefers deep, well-drained, semi-dry soil.
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery, Fruitopia Nursery humid subtropical climates, Ross Creek Tropicals Gympie, Sunshine Coast nurseries

varieties.name: Grafted Kyogle Selection
varieties.origin: Kyogle, New South Wales, Australia (subtropical selection)
varieties.traits: Medium-large fruit, heart-shaped, green skin, proven performer in marginal subtropical climate
varieties.flesh_fruit: White, juicy with sweet lemony taste (not sour like lemon), firm texture, less fibrous than seedlings
varieties.yield_notes: Good crop production, multiple fruits per season, bears within 2-3 years of grafting

varieties.name: Golden/Yellow Soursop Selection
varieties.origin: Rare selection available in Australian subtropical nurseries
varieties.traits: Large fruit, golden-yellow when ripe (distinguishing feature), spiny green skin that yellows at maturity
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet and aromatic, perfect blend of tropical fruits with berry hint, less acidic than standard green varieties
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer with proper care, typical soursop yield range

varieties.name: Smooth Flesh Selection
varieties.origin: Queensland selection grown from smooth-fleshed variety
varieties.traits: Heart-shaped fruit, typical rough green skin with soft spines
varieties.flesh_fruit: Notable for smoother, less fibrous flesh texture, juicy and slightly acid, creamy consistency
varieties.yield_notes: Standard soursop productivity
varieties.growing_notes: Seedling selection specifically chosen for superior flesh texture. Excellent for fresh consumption and processing. Superb when pureed with vanilla ice cream. Requires subtropical to tropical climate with frost protection.

varieties.name: Giant South American Selection
varieties.origin: South American seed-grown selection
varieties.traits: Large conical fruits, typically 7kg+, specimens exceeding 10kg possible on established trees
varieties.flesh_fruit: Firm, white, less fibrous than other commercially available varieties
varieties.yield_notes: Large individual fruit size compensates for typical soursop shy-bearing habit

varieties.name: Sunshine Coast Grafted Selection
varieties.origin: Sunshine Coast, Queensland selection
varieties.traits: Heart-shaped fruit, rough green skin with soft fleshy spines
varieties.flesh_fruit: Juicy, slightly acid, producing rich creamy thirst-quenching juice
varieties.yield_notes: Good cropping history in Sunshine Coast conditions

varieties.name: Mountain Soursop (Annona montana)
varieties.origin: Central and South America, naturalized in various tropical regions
varieties.traits: Round fruit with pale green-yellow skin when ripe, less spiky than regular soursop, fruit 10-15cm diameter
varieties.flesh_fruit: Lemon-colored, smooth flesh with milder flavor than regular soursop, somewhat inferior quality but acceptable
varieties.yield_notes: Produces within 2-3 years from seed, reliable bearer
varieties.growing_notes: MUCH HARDIER than regular soursop - tolerates temperatures down to -2°C, making it the BEST CHOICE for marginal humid subtropical climates areas. Will tolerate more cold than Annona muricata and can be used as rootstock for grafting regular soursop in colder regions. Adaptable to wide range of soils and dry conditions. Can grow in full sun or part shade, reaching 8m height.
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery, specialist subtropical nurseries

varieties.name: Mullatha / Mullu Chakka (Indian Common) 🌏
varieties.origin: Indigenous to South India, widely cultivated in tropical monsoon climates, Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh
varieties.traits: Large oval to heart-shaped fruits, dark green leathery skin with soft protruding spines, fruit weight 2-6kg
varieties.flesh_fruit: White, creamy, fibrous segments surrounding pithy core, sweet-sour flavor with musky aroma, contains 30-200 black seeds
varieties.yield_notes: Continuous flowering and fruiting, main season April-October in India, 12-24 fruits per tree

varieties.name: Sweet Type (Sirsak Ratu - Indonesian influence)
varieties.origin: Southeast Asian selection, cultivated in tropical monsoon climates through historical trade connections
varieties.traits: Small to medium fruit, few-seeded, typically 1-2kg
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sub-acid flavor (lower acidity), sweeter than common types, preferred for fresh eating
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate productivity, fewer but sweeter fruits
varieties.growing_notes: Similar to sweet types grown in Indonesia and Philippines. Predominant in a few tropical monsoon climates localities. Best for fresh consumption. Excellent adaptation to tropical monsoon conditions with high humidity during fruit set. Thrives in Tropical monsoon warm temperatures (21-32°C).
varieties.availability: Specialty tropical monsoon climates nurseries, inquire at local fruit tree sellers

varieties.name: Aguinaldo Type (Philippine Introduction)
varieties.origin: Philippines, introduced to India through tropical fruit exchange programs
varieties.traits: Medium fruit approximately 1kg, yellow-green peel
varieties.flesh_fruit: Juicy, sub-acid (moderately acidic), 78% of fruit weight is edible, approximately 70 seeds per fruit
varieties.yield_notes: Good edible portion ratio, reliable cropper in tropical conditions
varieties.growing_notes: Part of Philippines' major soursop production heritage (Western Visayas, Central Luzon leading regions). Adapted to similar tropical monsoon climate as tropical monsoon climates. Sub-acid types excellent for both fresh consumption and processing. Can be consumed fresh or used for juices and desserts.
varieties.availability: May be available through specialty importers or agricultural research stations in tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Davao Type (Philippine Introduction)
varieties.origin: Davao region, Philippines
varieties.traits: Larger fruit approximately 1.7kg, light green peel
varieties.flesh_fruit: Moderately juicy, pleasantly sub-acid, 82% of fruit weight is edible, approximately 82 seeds per fruit
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent edible portion (82%), good size fruit, reliable bearer
varieties.growing_notes: Higher edible percentage than Aguinaldo type. Philippines is major producer with similar tropical climate to tropical monsoon climates. Mindanao region (including Davao) known for quality soursop. Excellent for Tropical monsoon climate with high temperatures and humidity. Pleasant sub-acid flavor makes it versatile for fresh eating and processing.
varieties.availability: May be available through specialty importers or agricultural research stations in tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Seedling Selections (Kerala-Adapted)
varieties.origin: Local tropical monsoon climates selections from superior mother trees
varieties.traits: Variable, ranging from round to heart-shaped to angular, skin color from dark green to yellow-green at maturity
varieties.flesh_fruit: Quality varies from soft and juicy to firm and dry, flavor ranges from sweet to sub-acid to acid
varieties.yield_notes: Variable but adapted to local conditions, fruits within 2-3 years
varieties.growing_notes: tropical monsoon climates has diverse soursop genetic material grown on limited scale. Trees grow wild and cultivated in Southern India. Excellent adaptation to tropical monsoon climate. Thrives on coastal regions with tropical weather. Prefers well-drained, loamy soil with high humidity during fruit set. Continuous flowering with main season April-October.
varieties.availability: Local tropical monsoon climates nurseries, home garden exchanges, Greens of tropical monsoon climates, Golden Hills Farm

varieties.name: Crioula (Brazilian Introduction) 🏆
varieties.origin: Brazil - traditional variety with 100+ year history
varieties.traits: Variable fruit size, traditional Brazilian landrace characteristics
varieties.flesh_fruit: Traditional flavor profile, pulp properties vary within acceptable range for cultivar
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable production in tropical conditions

varieties.name: Blanca (Brazilian Introduction)
varieties.origin: Brazil
varieties.traits: 'Blanca' refers to white flesh characteristic, distinguishing feature from cultivar name
varieties.flesh_fruit: Notable white flesh quality, good pulp characteristics
varieties.yield_notes: Differs from other Brazilian varieties in size, shape, and yield characteristics

varieties.name: FAO II (Brazilian Introduction)
varieties.origin: Brazil - variety from FAO agricultural development programs
varieties.traits: Selected for specific commercial characteristics including fruit size and shape
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pulp properties optimized for commercial production and processing
varieties.yield_notes: Selected for improved productivity characteristics
varieties.growing_notes: Developed through FAO agricultural programs for tropical fruit improvement. Represents improved germplasm with enhanced commercial characteristics. Well-suited to tropical monsoon climate with high humidity and rainfall patterns similar to tropical monsoon climates.
varieties.availability: May be available through agricultural research institutions or FAO programs

varieties.name: Lisa (Brazilian Introduction)
varieties.origin: Brazil - 'Lisa' means 'smooth' in Portuguese, referring to texture characteristics
varieties.traits: Selected for smoother skin or flesh characteristics
varieties.flesh_fruit: Notable smooth texture, reduced fibrousness compared to other varieties

varieties.name: Morada (Brazilian Introduction)
varieties.origin: Brazil - 'Morada' means 'dwelling/residence' in Portuguese, traditional variety name
varieties.traits: Traditional Brazilian variety with distinct characteristics
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pulp properties differ significantly from other main Brazilian varieties
varieties.yield_notes: Yield characteristics vary from Crioula, Blanca, FAO II, and Lisa

varieties.name: Guanaba Azucaron (Central American Sweet) 🏆
varieties.origin: El Salvador - traditional sweet variety ('azucaron' means 'sugary' in Spanish)
varieties.traits: Sweet type fruit, lower acidity than acid types
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet (relatively low acid), excellent for fresh consumption and drinks
varieties.yield_notes: Typical soursop production levels

varieties.name: Guanabana Dulce (Dominican Sweet) 🏆
varieties.origin: Dominican Republic - traditional sweet variety ('dulce' means 'sweet' in Spanish)
varieties.traits: Most sought-after variety in Dominican Republic
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet (low acidity in relative sense), excellent flavor for fresh eating
varieties.yield_notes: Highly valued commercial variety in Dominican production

varieties.name: Cuban Fiberless (Guanabana Sin Fibre) 🏆
varieties.origin: Santiago de las Vegas Agricultural Experiment Station, Cuba (1920s-1950s)
varieties.traits: Medium-sized, yellow-green fruit, distinctive bluish-green foliage
varieties.flesh_fruit: FIBERLESS or very low fiber content (defining characteristic), smooth texture, less acid than typical varieties
varieties.yield_notes: Vegetatively propagated clone ensures consistent quality
varieties.growing_notes: HEIRLOOM STATUS - historic Cuban selection from 1920s-1950s era. Vegetatively propagated superior clone. Distinctive bluish-green foliage allows identification. Fiberless characteristic highly valued for processing and fresh consumption. Cuban agricultural expertise in tropical fruit improvement. Made its way to Florida in 1959. Ideal for Tropical monsoon tropical climate. Excellent for ice cream, smoothies, and juice production due to smooth texture.
varieties.availability: May be available through Cuban germplasm sources or propagated from existing Cuban Fiberless trees internationally

varieties.name: Bennett (Costa Rican Selection) 🏆
varieties.origin: Costa Rica, 1920 - named for G.S. Bennett, Agricultural Superintendent of United Fruit Company's Costa Rican Division
varieties.traits: Large, handsome fruit, described by Dr. Wilson Popenoe as most fruitful tree seen
varieties.flesh_fruit: Excellent quality, superior characteristics for commercial production
varieties.yield_notes: Exceptional productivity - most fruitful soursop tree documented in 1920

varieties.name: Indonesian Selections (Sirsak)
varieties.origin: Indonesia - world's largest soursop producer
varieties.traits: Diverse selections adapted to Indonesian tropical conditions, variable fruit size and shape
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet-fruited and sour-fruited types available, quality varies by selection
varieties.yield_notes: Indonesia produces hundreds of thousands of tons annually, indicating excellent productivity
varieties.growing_notes: Indonesia is world's TOP soursop producer with optimal growing conditions. Major production regions: Java, Sumatra, Bali, Sulawesi, Kalimantan. Tropical climate with fertile volcanic soil creates ideal conditions. Trees propagated using seeds or grafting, with grafted plants offering earlier fruiting. Extensive post-harvest handling expertise. Climate highly similar to tropical monsoon climates - tropical, high rainfall, fertile soil. Indonesian cultivation practices directly applicable to tropical monsoon climates production.
varieties.availability: May be available through Indonesian agricultural institutions or Southeast Asian germplasm exchange

varieties.name: Thai Selections (Thurian Thet/Thurian Khaek)
varieties.origin: Thailand
varieties.traits: Thai selections adapted to Southeast Asian tropical conditions
varieties.flesh_fruit: Used extensively in Thai desserts, ice creams, and beverages
varieties.yield_notes: Produced primarily for domestic consumption and regional export

varieties.name: Malaysian Selections (Durian Belanda)
varieties.origin: Malaysia
varieties.traits: Malaysian selections with tropical adaptation
varieties.flesh_fruit: Quality characteristics suited to fresh consumption and processing
varieties.yield_notes: Contributes to regional production, smaller scale than Indonesia
varieties.growing_notes: Malaysia grows soursop on smaller scale than Indonesia but with similar climate advantages. Tropical climate with high humidity and rainfall year-round. Well-drained soil requirements similar to tropical monsoon climates conditions. Southeast Asian cultivation experience valuable for tropical monsoon climates growers. Climate characteristics closely match tropical monsoon climates monsoon patterns.
varieties.availability: May be available through Malaysian agricultural institutions or regional exchanges

varieties.name: Vietnamese Selections (Mang Cau Xiem)
varieties.origin: Vietnam
varieties.traits: Vietnamese tropical adaptations
varieties.flesh_fruit: Quality suited to Vietnamese fresh fruit markets
varieties.yield_notes: Supports local and regional markets
varieties.growing_notes: Vietnam cultivates soursop with tropical monsoon climate matching tropical monsoon climates. Strong agricultural sector with tropical fruit expertise. Climate includes distinct wet season with heavy rainfall similar to tropical monsoon climates monsoon. High humidity and warm temperatures year-round. Vietnamese cultivation techniques applicable to tropical monsoon climates conditions.
varieties.availability: May be available through Vietnamese agricultural institutions

varieties.name: Mexican Selections (Guanabana)
varieties.origin: Mexico - world's leading producer and exporter
varieties.traits: Diverse selections optimized for commercial production and export, variable fruit sizes
varieties.flesh_fruit: Quality optimized for frozen pulp production and fresh fruit export
varieties.yield_notes: Leading world production with advanced cultivation techniques
varieties.growing_notes: Mexico is WORLD'S LEADING PRODUCER and primary exporter of soursop products (frozen pulp and fresh fruit). Extensive commercial cultivation experience and advanced production techniques. Main season June-September in Mexican growing regions. While some Mexican regions are drier than tropical monsoon climates, coastal tropical areas have similar conditions. Mexican germplasm represents commercial-quality selections with proven export standards. Expertise in post-harvest handling and processing valuable for tropical monsoon climates production development.
varieties.availability: May be available through Mexican agricultural institutions or major commercial nurseries

varieties.name: Puerto Rican Selections 🏆
varieties.origin: Puerto Rico - 14 distinct types cataloged by Agricultural Experiment Station
varieties.traits: Wide range cataloged: sweet, sub-acid, and acid types; subdivided by shape (round, heart-shaped, oblong, angular); further classified by flesh consistency (soft/juicy to firm/dry)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Variable - from soft and juicy to firm and comparatively dry
varieties.yield_notes: Historical production of 99,790 kg in 1977, indicating significant cultivation

varieties.name: FIRST CHOICE: Mullatha/Mullu Chakka (indigenous) - perfectly adapted, medicinal value, local availability
